source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure 'external scripts enabled' Database Flag for Cloud SQL SQL Server Instance Is Set to 'off'

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure 'external scripts enabled' Database Flag for Cloud SQL SQL Server Instance Is Set to 'off'
input_row.description: It is recommended to set external scripts enabled database flag for Cloud SQL SQL Server instance to off
input_row.rationale: external scripts enabled enable the execution of scripts with certain remote language extensions. This property is OFF by default. When Advanced Analytics Services is installed, setup can optionally set this property to true. As the External Scripts Enabled feature allows scripts external to SQL such as files located in an R library to be executed, which could adversely affect the security of the system, hence this should be disabled. This recommendation is applicable to SQL Server database instances. Impact: Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance to open its Instance Overview page 3. Ensure the database flag external scripts enabled that has been set is listed under the Database flags section. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Ensure the below command returns off for every Cloud SQL SQL Server database instance gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="external scripts enabled")|.value'  In the output, database flags are listed under the settings as the collection databaseFlags.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the SQL Server instance for which you want to enable to database flag. 3. Click Edit. 4. Scroll down to the Flags section. 5. To set a flag that has not been set on the instance before, click Add item, choose the flag external scripts enabled from the drop-down menu, and set its value to off. 6. Click Save to save your changes. 7. Confirm your changes under Flags on the Overview page. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Configure the external scripts enabled database flag for every Cloud SQL SQL Server database instance using the below command. gcloud sql instances patch <INSTANCE_NAME> --database-flags "external scripts enabled"=off Note : This command will overwrite all database flags previously set. To keep those and add new ones, include the values for all flags you want set on the instance; any flag not specifically included is set to its default value. For flags that do not take a value, specify the flag name followed by an equals sign ("="). Default Value: By default external scripts enabled is off References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/database-engine/configure- windows/external-scripts-enabled-server-configuration-option?view=sql-server- ver15 2. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags 3. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/advanced- analytics/concepts/security?view=sql-server-ver15 4. https://www.stigviewer.com/stig/ms_sql_server_2016_instance/2018-03- 09/finding/V-79347 Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability, and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines." Note : Configuring the above flag restarts the Cloud SQL instance.
input_row.impact: Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.references: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/database-engine/configure- windows/external-scripts-enabled-server-configuration-option?view=sql-server- ver15 2. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags 3. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/advanced- analytics/concepts/security?view=sql-server-ver15 4. https://www.stigviewer.com/stig/ms_sql_server_2016_instance/2018-03- 09/finding/V-79347 Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability, and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines." Note : Configuring the above flag restarts the Cloud SQL instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- ✓ Can GCP APIs query this property? → Automated
- ✓ Is this a standard GCP service property (encryption, logging, access control, network rules)? → Automated
- ✓ Does Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK have methods to check this? → Automated
- ✓ Does GCP CLI have commands to retrieve this? → Automated
- ✓ Does audit mention Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI commands? → Automated

In this control, we need to ensure that the "external scripts enabled" database flag for Cloud SQL SQL Server instances is set to "off." This can be verified using the Google Cloud CLI, making it fully automatable.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_external_scripts_enabled_flag_checked

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **Command:** Use `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json` and parse the output using a command like `jq` to check the value of the database flag.
- **API Call:** Enumerate all Cloud SQL SQL Server instances using `gcloud sql instances list --filter="DATABASE_VERSION:SQLSERVER*"`.
- **Properties to Validate:** Check the `settings.databaseFlags` collection for any flag named `"external scripts enabled"` and ensure its value is `"off"`.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - Pass: The flag `"external scripts enabled"` is either not set or set to `"off"`.
  - Fail: The flag is set to `"on"` or any value other than `"off"`.
- **Resources Enumerated:** All instances of Cloud SQL configured with SQL Server.
- **Importance:** This is important as enabling external scripts could introduce security vulnerabilities by allowing the execution of potentially harmful scripts.

By verifying these settings through automation, we ensure consistent and efficient compliance audits across GCP SQL instances.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. This control can be fully assessed using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). The relevant property (`external scripts enabled` flag) is exposed via the Cloud SQL Admin API and is retrievable using `gcloud sql instances describe`.
- **GCP SDK/CLI Capability:**  
  The `gcloud sql instances describe` command accurately retrieves the `settings.databaseFlags` for each Cloud SQL instance. Filtering for SQL Server instances using `gcloud sql instances list --filter="DATABASE_VERSION:SQLSERVER*"` is also correct.
- **Other GCP Tools:**  
  GCP Config (now part of Config Connector) does not natively monitor Cloud SQL database flags at this granularity, so the approach using `gcloud`/API is appropriate.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 9/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  - The described process is technically correct: enumerate SQL Server instances, describe each, and check the `settings.databaseFlags` for the relevant flag.
  - The use of `jq` (or similar JSON parsing) is standard for automation.
- **gcloud/CLI Commands:**  
  - `gcloud sql instances list --filter="DATABASE_VERSION:SQLSERVER*"` is valid.
  - `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json` is valid.
- **Program Name Convention:**  
  - The name `gcp_sql_instance_external_scripts_enabled_flag_checked` is descriptive, but could be more precise (see Improvements).
- **Completeness:**  
  - The assessment correctly notes that the flag must be either absent (default is "off") or explicitly set to "off" to pass.
  - One minor omission: The flag is named `external scripts enabled` (with spaces) in GCP, but in the API/CLI output, it appears as `external scripts enabled` in the `databaseFlags` array. The parsing logic should ensure it matches the exact flag name and is case-sensitive.
- **Edge Cases:**  
  - The assessment does not mention handling instances where the `databaseFlags` property is missing or empty (which should be interpreted as "off" for this flag).

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Program Name:**  
  - Suggest: `gcp_sql_sqlserver_instance_external_scripts_enabled_off`  
    This is more precise, indicating both the engine (SQL Server) and the required state ("off").
- **Automation Details:**  
  - Explicitly mention that the flag name is `"external scripts enabled"` (with spaces) and should be checked exactly as it appears in the API output.
  - Clarify handling of missing/empty `databaseFlags` (should be treated as "off"/compliant).
- **Alternative API Methods:**  
  - The Cloud SQL Admin API's `instances.list` and `instances.get` endpoints can be used directly for programmatic checks, which may be relevant for organizations using custom automation outside of `gcloud`.
- **Security Best Practice:**  
  - Recommend logging non-compliant instances and optionally providing a remediation script to set the flag to "off".
- **Documentation:**  
  - Reference the [Cloud SQL SQL Server flags documentation](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags) for completeness.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is technically sound and aligns with GCP API capabilities and security best practices. Minor improvements are suggested for clarity and completeness, but the core approach is correct.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify (minor improvements)**

- **Keep as Automated.**
- **Modify:**  
  - Update the program name to `gcp_sql_sqlserver_instance_external_scripts_enabled_off` for clarity.
  - Add explicit handling for missing/empty `databaseFlags`.
  - Clarify that the flag name is `"external scripts enabled"` (case and spacing sensitive).
  - Optionally, reference the Cloud SQL Admin API as an alternative to `gcloud`.

---

**Summary Table**

| Section              | Decision/Score | Notes                                                                                   |
|----------------------|---------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation  | AGREE         | Fully automatable via GCP APIs/gcloud.                                                  |
| Technical Accuracy   | 9/10          | Correct commands and logic; minor edge case handling could be improved.                 |
| Improvements         | -             | Program name, edge case handling, flag name clarity, API reference.                     |
| Confidence           | HIGH          | Assessment is robust and aligns with GCP best practices.                                |
| Final Recommendation | Modify        | Minor improvements for clarity and completeness; keep as Automated.                     |

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the "external scripts enabled" flag for Cloud SQL SQL Server instances is fully accessible via the GCP Cloud SQL Admin API and can be programmatically audited using the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). The audit steps explicitly reference the use of `gcloud` commands, and the property is exposed in the `settings.databaseFlags` field for each instance. No manual inspection or console-only steps are required.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_sql_sqlserver_instance_external_scripts_enabled_flag_off

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Cloud SQL Instances (SQL Server)
- API/CLI Method:  
  - List SQL Server instances:  
    `gcloud sql instances list --filter="DATABASE_VERSION:SQLSERVER*"`
  - Describe each instance:  
    `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json`
- Properties to Check:  
  - `settings.databaseFlags[]` (look for a flag named `external scripts enabled`)
- Pass Criteria:  
  - The flag `external scripts enabled` is either not set (default is off) or explicitly set to `off`
- Fail Criteria:  
  - The flag `external scripts enabled` is set to any value other than `off` (e.g., `on`)

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements between the two AI opinions; both agreed on automation and the technical approach. The final program name has been made more precise to reflect GCP, SQL Server, the instance resource, and the specific flag and intent.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
